title: Running a systemd container with rootfull podman but using uidmap fails because the cgroupv2 filesystem is owned by an unmapped user
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Running a systemd container with rootfull podman but using `uidmap` fails because the cgroupv2 filesystem is owned by an unmapped user.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build a simple systemd-based container image:

```Containerfile
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:33
RUN dnf install -y systemd && \
    dnf clean all
CMD [ "/usr/sbin/init" ]
```

`# podman image build --rm -t localhost/fedora-systemd:latest .`

2. Run the container, using `--tty` to see the output from systemd.

```shell
# podman container run --rm --tty --uidmap=0:1000:1 --uidmap=1:100000:65536 --gidmap=0:1000:1 --gidmap=1:100000:65536 --name fedora-systemd01 localhost/fedora-systemd:latest
systemd v246.15-1.fc33 running in system mode. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +ZSTD +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization container-other.
Detected architecture x86-64.

Welcome to Fedora 33 (Container Image)!

Set hostname to <c762c519fb38>.
Failed to create /init.scope control group: Permission denied
Failed to allocate manager object: Permission denied
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object.
Exiting PID 1...
```

**Describe the results you received:**

systemd fails to run because it can't access cgroupv2.

The uid (and gid) map look correct:

```shell
# podman container run --rm --uidmap=0:1000:1 --uidmap=1:100000:65536 --gidmap=0:1000:1 --gidmap=1:100000:65536 --name fedora-systemd01 localhost/fedora-systemd:latest cat /proc/1/uid_map
         0       1000          1
         1     100000      65536
```

But the uid and gid owners of the cgroupv2 mount do not:

```shell
# podman container run --rm --uidmap=0:1000:1 --uidmap=1:100000:65536 --gidmap=0:1000:1 --gidmap=1:100000:65536 --name fedora-systemd01 localhost/fedora-systemd:latest ls -ln /sys/fs/cgroup/
total 0
-r--r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.controllers
-r--r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.events
-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.freeze
--w-------. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.kill
-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.max.depth
-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.max.descendants
-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 03:21 cgroup.procs
...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected this to work. The uid (and gid) mapping looks exactly the same as when using rootless podman (with the exact same image). Running this container with rootless podman works. Running this container with rootfull podman without the uid and gid mapping options also works. For both of these cases, the uid and gid owners of the cgroupv2 mount are 0:0 (notice the below command is for rootless podman):

```shell
$ podman container run --rm --name fedora-systemd01 localhost/fedora-systemd:latest ls -ln /sys/fs/cgroup/
total 0
-r--r--r--. 1 0 0 0 Nov  4 03:03 cgroup.controllers
-r--r--r--. 1 0 0 0 Nov  4 03:03 cgroup.events
-rw-r--r--. 1 0 0 0 Nov  4 03:03 cgroup.freeze
--w-------. 1 0 0 0 Nov  4 03:03 cgroup.kill
-rw-r--r--. 1 0 0 0 Nov  4 03:03 cgroup.max.depth
...
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.14
Built:        Thu Sep 23 14:22:19 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

Rootfull:


Rootless:

```

  idMappings:

      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.15.0-1.el8.elrepo.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 60079386624
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.4.0+942+d25aada8.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

I've checked the troubleshooting guide and many other github issues. I've not tried the most recent version of Podman, or crun.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Base system is centos 8, but running the latest elrepo kernel. systemd configured to use the unified hierarchy. SELinux is in permissive mode.

```
# uname -r
5.15.0-1.el8.elrepo.x86_64
# systemctl --version
systemd 239 (239-45.el8)
+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=legacy
# cat /proc/cmdline 
... systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=1
# ls -l /sys/fs/cgroup/
total 0
-r--r--r--.   1 root root 0 Nov  1 10:37 cgroup.controllers
-rw-r--r--.   1 root root 0 Nov  1 10:37 cgroup.max.depth
-rw-r--r--.   1 root root 0 Nov  1 10:37 cgroup.max.descendants
-rw-r--r--.   1 root root 0 Nov  1 10:37 cgroup.procs
...
# mount | grep cgroup
cgroup2 on /sys/fs/cgroup type cgroup2 (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel)
# sestatus
SELinux status:                 enabled
SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
Loaded policy name:             targeted
Current mode:                   permissive
Mode from config file:          enforcing
Policy MLS status:              enabled
Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
Memory protection checking:     actual (secure)
Max kernel policy version:      33
```

The system has a typical subuid (and gid) map setup:

```shell
# cat /etc/subuid
jeroy:100000:65536
containers:200000:65536
```

comments.body:
I just tried with `crun version 1.2.34-e2dd` (compiled from git main) and the result is about the same. Some of the controllers in the cgroupv2 mount are owned by uid 0 (container userns), but most are not:

```shell
# ls -ln /sys/fs/cgroup/
total 0
-r--r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 17:10 cgroup.controllers

-rw-r--r--. 1     0     0 0 Nov  4 17:10 cgroup.procs

-rw-r--r--. 1     0     0 0 Nov  4 17:10 cgroup.subtree_control

-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 Nov  4 17:10 cgroup.type
```

The `/sys/fs/cgroup` mount is wrong for CGv2 - it's being mounted as `ro` but Libpod should always be mounting it as `rw` when the container is in systemd mode.
Ah, we're not in systemd mode... Can you try with `--systemd=always`?
Ah man, I was running the container with an override on the command, because it does not start if it is systemd. Yeah, let me reattach this data with that flag.

OK, same problem with systemd mode forced.

Ah, it actually works fine with crun 1.2.34-e2dd. I should have paid more attention to the cgroup directory.

crun does the following:
- The cgroup root directory is chown'ed to the container uid/gid 0.
- Controllers listed in `/sys/kernel/cgroup/delegate` are chown'ed to the container uid/gid 0.

This is enough for systemd. The code is here: [chown_cgroups](https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/main/src/libcrun/cgroup.c#L659). It was introduced a while ago by https://github.com/containers/crun/commit/1c778d4543aac28db380f77f00c66d73885aa07b, and further refined to respect delegate by https://github.com/containers/crun/commit/982813c3bd4de945ac1272dd99eff6bb0621fb97.

I don't really see any similar logic in runc. So I suppose I should open a bug on it?
And it's pretty clear from https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/cgroups.7.html that runc is not handling delegation properly.
Could you open an issue with runc?

> Could you open an issue with runc?

Yep will do. I've in fact been digging into runc and hoping to write the PR myself.

there is already an open PR for runc: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3057

I am closing the issue since there is nothing we can do in Podman
